summary: Frontend adds an owner-only Delete button on SearchRentalResultCard with a confirmation dialog and calls actor.deleteRental via a new React Query mutation hook. SearchRentalPage tracks deleted rentals locally to remove them immediately from the grid. Backend changes are shown but the diff is truncated before any deleteRental implementation/authorization logic can be verified.

missing_requirements.requirement: Every rental card includes a "Delete" button element in the card layout (even if conditionally hidden).
missing_requirements.reason: In the component, the Delete button is only rendered when `isOwner` is truthy; for non-owners it is not present in the layout/DOM at all, rather than being present-but-hidden.

missing_requirements.requirement: Backend must enforce owner-only authorization for rental deletion and ensure deleting a rental does not automatically delete/modify any Orders.
missing_requirements.reason: The backend file shown is truncated before any `deleteRental` function/authorization check or any order-side effects can be confirmed from the provided diff summary.

hallucinated_features.description: Additional confirmation dialog title text "Delete Rental" (extra user-facing text beyond the required exact confirmation message).
hallucinated_features.reason: Spec only constrains the confirmation popup message to exactly "Are you sure you want to delete this product?"; the diff adds an additional title string not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Alert-based error messaging ("You are not authorized to delete this rental" / "Failed to delete rental. Please try again.").
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested in requirements; introduces additional user-facing text/behavior for the delete workflow beyond the specified confirmation prompt.

hallucinated_features.description: Local UI state tracking of deleted rentals via a `deletedRentals` Set on SearchRentalPage to filter results.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not explicitly requested (though it supports immediate removal); it is an additional mechanism beyond ‘update local/UI state and/or invalidate caches’.
